content:
2 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

On 1st May 2026, NIST Center of AI published an evaluation of DeepSeek V4 Pro, with a headline capability chart showing US models clearly pulling away from Chinese models.
The three benchmarks where DeepSeek falls furthest behind are

• CTF-Archive-Diamond (a CAISI-developed cyber benchmark)

• PortBench (a CAISI-developed, non-public software engineering eval whose in-depth description hasn’t been released yet)

• ARC-AGI-2
Nobody outside CAISI can reproduce CTF-Archive-Diamond or PortBench.
A cursory look at the other benchmarks selected for this chart shows saturation.

• OTIS-AIME-2025 scores run 90–100%

• PUMaC 2024 runs 93–96%

• SMT 2025 runs 92–99%

• GPQA-Diamond is close behind at 87–96%.
This report came less than 3 weeks after the Stanford HAI 2026 AI Index
https://hai.stanford.edu/assets/files/ai_index_report_2026.pdf
Stanford found the US-China performance gap to be effectively closed, with Anthropic’s top model leading the best Chinese models by just 2.7% as of March 2026, while CAISI found DeepSeek’s best model lagging the frontier by about 8 months.
In my view DeepSeek V4 Pro isn’t a strong model. But in the last couple of months, there have been releases of two strong models Kimi K2.6 and GLM-5.2.
CAISI have not published evaluations of these models, so at the time of writing, their latest communication presents the view that US models are continuing to pull away.
